title: Network Design Engineer
description: Note: By applying to this position you will have an opportunity to share your preferred working location from the following: **Dublin, Ireland; London, UK**.### **Minimum qualifications:** * Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, a related field, or equivalent practical experience. * 3 years of experience with network routing protocols, design and troubleshooting, with network equipment providers. * 3 years of experience in Dense Wavelength Division Multiplexing (DWDM) or Transmission Control Protocol/Internet Protocol (TCP/IP) or Network system design operations. * Experience in one or more programming languages such as C\+\+, Go, or Python. * Experience in coding/scripting languages such as Go or Python, and SQL scripts. * Experience with Content Distribution Networks (CDN), IP transit/peering, dark fiber, colocation, optical transport, and cloud technologies. ### **Preferred qualifications:** * Experience with planning and designing networks (e.g., multiple sites, multiple products, etc.). * Experience in telecommunications at carrier\-class scale, with experience of DWDM optical and IP networks, as well as network automation scripting using Go or Python, and SQL scripts. * Experience with scaled capacity augment/migration projects in telecommunication, hyperscalar, data center or colocation networking environment. * Experience with defining system or process requirements, building, integrating, or delivering layer 1, layer 2 or layer 3 network systems. * Experience with forecasting and planning, and design tools, workflow automation tooling and improvement, automation scripts. **About the job** ----------------- Google is proud to boast a network that provides service to millions of Internet users around the world. The Network Engineering team is responsible for operating that network reliably and at scale. As a member of the team, you have a direct impact on design and feature enhancements to keep our systems running smoothly. You also ensure that network operations are safe and efficient by monitoring network performance, coordinating planned maintenance, adjusting hardware components and responding to network connectivity issues. Google's complex network generates a constant stream of challenges which require you to continually be innovative with an evolving set of technologies. Keeping the network reliable ensures that our users stay connected with our suite of applications, products and services. In this role, you will plan and design the world's largest Google's production global network. You will be involved in a series of dynamic capacity augments, migrations, and new technology introductions globally to transition to Alphanet. You will execute and build upon our goal of an incredible optical, edge, core and campus network infrastructure for Alphanet to support continuous growing customer and ML demand. You will ensure that the network is efficiently built on\-time while meeting our reliability and policy requirements. You will develop network planning and design best practices, processes and tooling. You will promote, create and contribute to automation tooling/scripts into network planning and design. You will drive the software automation roadmap to improve the quality and reduce the cycle time for network planning and design.Behind everything our users see online is the architecture built by the Technical Infrastructure team to keep it running. From developing and maintaining our data centers to building the next\-generation of Google platforms, we make Google's product portfolio possible. We're proud to be our engineers' engineers and love voiding warranties by taking things apart so we can rebuild them. We keep our networks up and running, ensuring our users have the best and fastest experience possible.Individual pay is determined by factors including job\-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Ireland: €100000 \- €103000 (EUR) \+ 15% bonus target \+ equity \+ benefits Learn more about benefits at Google.**Responsibilities** -------------------- * Review Google's network architecture standards and design global infrastructure that meets capacity requirements and service\-level objectives (SLOs) while optimizing the capital expenditures and operating expenditures (CapEx/OpEx) balance. * Incorporate newly New Product Introduction (NPI)’d solutions into plans and designs, ensure planning and design toolsets and business processes are ready for NPIs. * Evolve the business processes throughout their life\-cycle (e.g., input gathering, generation, validation, publication, handoff). * Support establishment and maintenance of business practices and policies in network planning and design (e.g., architectural/topology, hardware life\-cycle, capacity/utilization target), improve performance, reliability, and reduce risk. * Create and promote automation tooling/scripts into network planning and design, contribute to the automation roadmap of all automatable tasks performed by the team, substantially reducing cycle time and improving quality and consistency. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form.

title: Network Engineer, Deployment & Support
description: Meta is looking for a Network Deployment \& Support Engineer who will be responsible for the deployment and support of one of the world’s largest and most complex networks. As a member of this team, you will have a unique opportunity to be involved in projects related to implementation \& scaling of Meta’s network. You will need to work with a constant sense of urgency continually growing a constantly evolving network. This team is responsible for planning and scaling one of the world's largest backbone and edge networks. As a Network Deployment Engineer, you will propose new ideas, gather constructive feedback from others, and push forward to make these ideas a reality. Network Engineer, Deployment \& Support Responsibilities: * Deploy and support our global production including edge, backbone, data center, campus, and content delivery network (CDN) infrastructure * Work cross\-functionally with project management, network engineering to generate implementation plans including Method of Procedure (MOP) and Bill of Materials (BOM), Engineering Design Packages (EDP), Scope of Works (SOW), Network equipment and server Rack Face Elevations (RFE) * Troubleshoot and resolve complex network issues, collaborate as needed to ensure timely resolution * Provide technical leadership and guidance during network deployment and migration activities in our data centers and POPs, including in the areas of facility power, cooling, rack layout and cable management, physical equipment installation, network equipment configuration, testing and handoff for production traffic * Drive problem management initiatives to solve complex issues that span multiple organizations. Initiatives exist globally across technical infrastructure reliability, policy, security, compliance, and business strategy * Help drive and align on workflow management tool requirements \- efficiency and automation roadmaps * Manage key vendor relationships including contract negotiations, pricing, and evaluation of current and potential partnerships * Manage partner engineering team relationships to enable the team(s) to effectively meet business needs * Develop and maintain technical documentation for network infrastructure and services * Provide input to the scaling and evolution of the team to meet the projected network demands * Up to 10% to 30% of travel Minimum Qualifications: * Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, relevant technical field, or equivalent practical experience * 5\+ years of IP networking experience with deploying, migrating and supporting service providers, data center, and/or enterprise network infrastructure * Experience configuring and troubleshooting IP routing/switching technologies and protocols including TCP/IP, BGP, ISIS, MPLS, RSVP and SDN * Experience coordinating and executing hardware installation and configuration * Hands\-on experience with enterprise and carrier class routing, switching, and optical transport platforms * Knowledge of data center design and operational best practices * Experience with Linux OS/networking * Experience analyzing situations and utilizing problem solving experiences under pressure * Proven experience executing network deployment and/or migration projects * Experience interacting with vendors and external parties to drive projects to completion Preferred Qualifications: * Experience working in a multi\-vendor network environment * Experience with field\-based work in Collocation Facilities, Internet Exchanges, and Central Offices * Scripting experience with Python or Perl * Network certifications such as CCIE, CCNP or JNCIP * Experience with process improvement and systems development, leveraging automation to streamline repetitive workflows * Demonstrated ability to integrate AI tools to optimize/redesign workflows and drive measurable impact (e.g., efficiency gains, quality improvements) * Experience adhering to and implementing responsible, ethical AI practices (e.g., risk assessment, bias mitigation, quality and accuracy reviews) * Demonstrated ongoing AI skill development (e.g., prompt/context engineering, agent orchestration) and staying current with emerging AI technologies About Meta: Meta builds technologies that help people connect, find communities, and grow businesses. When Facebook launched in 2004, it changed the way people connect. Apps like Messenger, Instagram and WhatsApp further empowered billions around the world. Now, Meta is moving beyond 2D screens toward immersive experiences like augmented and virtual reality to help build the next evolution in social technology. People who choose to build their careers by building with us at Meta help shape a future that will take us beyond what digital connection makes possible today—beyond the constraints of screens, the limits of distance, and even the rules of physics. Individual compensation is determined by skills, qualifications, experience, and location. Compensation details listed in this posting reflect the base hourly rate, monthly rate, or annual salary only, and do not include bonus, equity or sales incentives, if applicable. In addition to base compensation, Meta offers benefits. Learn more about benefits at Meta.

title: New Business & Seller Growth Executive - TikTok Shop, French Speaker (Third-party Associate)
description: **Responsibilities** TikTok Shop is an innovative marketplace fully integrated into TikTok. It brings together online sellers, buyers and creators \- all within TikTok to build a one\-stop, seamless shopping experience and bringing more commerce solutions that further enable our community to discover and engage with what they love. We are seeking a driven and proactive Sales Development Representative (SDR) to be a critical engine for our expansion. This is a high\-impact, fixed\-term contract where you will identify, reach out, and onboard the next wave of high\-potential sellers. And later work with these sellers to grow their GMV (Gross Merchandise Value) on our platform in close collaboration with our cross\-functional teams. The right candidate would have Sales Development acumen and experience. If you thrive on autonomy, possess exceptional communication and presentation skills, and are ready to take full ownership of high\-stakes goals in an evolving, high\-velocity environment, we want to hear from you. * Proactively conduct outbound prospecting via multi\-channel (email, phone, social) outbound campaigns to source and qualify high\-potential seller\-partners. * Lead initial pitches to understand seller needs, translating the unique value of TikTok Shop into compelling, tailored propositions for them. * Assess seller fit to confirm potential partners, ensuring strong platform fit, identifying the seller growth strategies, and clear pathways to GMV growth. * Support end\-to\-end seller onboarding, registration, account setup, and activation of new sellers. * Collaborate with internal teams to ensure continued support for onboarded sellers. Including merchant navigation through their growth on the platform. * Meet and exceed monthly/quarterly targets related to seller acquisition and seller GMV growth within a 3\-6 months period. * Understand the TikTok Shop platform, the competition, and our unique positioning in the industry. **Qualifications** Minimum Qualifications: * Proven B2B Sales Acumen: Of successful B2B sales development (SDR/BDR) or full\-cycle sales experience. * Communication Excellence: Exceptional command of English \& French, both written and verbal, for external negotiations and presentations. * Self\-Driven \& Organised: Demonstrated strong time management, organisational skills, and the ability to operate autonomously in a remote\-first setting. * Contract Commitment: Availability for a 6 month fixed\-term contract, with opportunity for extension based on impact and results. Preferred Qualifications * eCommerce experience: previous direct experience within a high\-growth eCommerce, online marketplace, or platform business is a strong advantage. * Startup passion: Experience operating effectively within a hyper\-scaling, fluid, or startup\-like environment. Important Note: Please be advised that this job posting is on behalf of a third\-party agency. This is a 6 month temporary assignment managed by a third\-party agency, who will be your employer. While you may be assigned to work at TikTok, you will not be a TikTok employee. All contractual terms, including payroll and benefits, will be handled by a third\-party agency. By applying, you agree that the information provided in your application may be processed and retained by TikTok for recruitment purposes and shared with a third\-party agency in accordance with TikTok's Applicant Privacy Notice https://lifeattiktok.com/legal/privacy Job Information **About TikTok** TikTok is the leading destination for short\-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo. Why Join Us Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy \- a mission we work towards every day. We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us. Diversity \& Inclusion TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.

title: New Business Sales Consultant
description: **Role Introduction** Join our growing Legal vertical as a New Business Sales Executive and become part of a fast\-expanding team helping shape the future of the legal industry. Every day, thousands of legal professionals across the UK and Republic of Ireland rely on our technology to work smarter, faster, and more efficiently — with over 3,000 law firms already trusting OneAdvanced to power innovation across their businesses. With an already established presence in the Republic of Ireland, this is an exciting opportunity for a driven and ambitious sales professional who thrives on building relationships, uncovering opportunities, and delivering real value to clients. You’ll work with warm leads and prospective customers to introduce a range of market\-leading legal solutions, while also cross\-selling the wider suite of OneAdvanced products and services. If you’re proactive, commercially minded, and passionate about putting customers first, we’d love to hear from you. **What You Will Do** * Generating and converting new B2B sales opportunities within the legal sector in Republic of Ireland, supported by a local Sales Development Representative * Building and managing a strong sales pipeline while consistently achieving revenue targets * Leading client discovery sessions, proposals, product presentations, and commercial negotiations * Developing trusted relationships with legal professionals both remotely and face\-to\-face * Identifying opportunities to introduce additional OneAdvanced solutions across the customer base * Maintaining accurate CRM records and tracking sales performance effectively **What You Will Have** * Experience in B2B sales or business development (tech/software experience is beneficial but not essential) * A confident communicator with strong negotiation and closing skills * Comfortable engaging and influencing senior stakeholders * Commercially aware with a strategic approach to sales * Strong presentation and relationship\-building abilities * Someone who enjoys working in a fast\-paced, ambitious, growth\-focused environment Why Join Us? * Be part of a rapidly growing business with strong market momentum * Work with innovative solutions that make a real impact in the legal sector * Join a supportive and ambitious team culture * Excellent opportunities for progression and personal development **What We Do For You** **Wellbeing that means something** * 26 days’ annual leave \+ public holidays, with the option to buy up to 4 additional days * Private medical insurance for everyone, with no medical history exclusions * Exceptional family leave: 26 weeks fully paid maternity/adoption leave, 4 weeks fully paid paternity leave, and 9 weeks fully paid parents leave * Up to 13 weeks full pay \+ 13 weeks half pay for sickness absence * 24/7 Employee Assistance Programme and annual flu vaccination * Hybrid working designed to support collaboration, flexibility, and wellbeing **Financial Benefits That Have Your Back** * Company\-wide performance bonus scheme * Pension with 5% matched company contributions * Income protection providing up to 75% of salary for up to 5 years * Life assurance worth 4x your salary from day one **Grow your career with us** * Leadership development, future manager programmes, and ongoing learning opportunities * Real\-time feedback, goal setting, and career development through our talent platform **Making a Difference** * 1 paid volunteering day each year * MatchIt! fundraising scheme, with company contributions towards causes you care about **Plus plenty of flexible benefits through RewardHub, including:** * Cycle to Work scheme * Discounted gym memberships through Epassi * Retail and lifestyle discounts via Perks at Work * Will\-writing services and additional holiday purchase options **Who We Are** At OneAdvanced, we are at the forefront of delivering sector\-focused technology solutions that simplify complexity, drive meaningful progress, and help build a fairer, more inclusive society. We’re much more than a software company. We deliver SaaS workflow applications and IT services that power organisations across Education, Government, Healthcare, Legal, Manufacturing, Housing, Retail, and more. OneAdvanced is one of the UK’s largest business software and services companies. Based in Birmingham (The Mailbox), operating across the UK, Ireland, India, and Australia. Our secure, scalable platform, including OneAdvanced AI, our private AI service for UK organisations, powers connectivity and innovation across critical sectors. Alongside our software are our IT services, including hosting, managed services, and application modernisation. We strive to create an inclusive workplace that drives innovation and collaboration, championing diverse perspectives and ideas. Our Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) strategy is embedded in everything we do, guiding us to create meaningful impact for our people, our customers and the planet. Join us and become part of a team that’s powering the world of work and making a real difference. Learn more at **www.oneadvanced.com**

title: Ocean Freight Apprentice

title: PLATFORM SUPPORT ENGINEER
description: **FULL TIME** **Dublin, Ireland** Réalta Technologies are searching for a Platform Support Engineer to support our Pharmaceutical client based in Dublin. This candidate will provide technical ownership and ongoing support for manufacturing digital platforms within a regulated pharmaceutical environment. The successful candidate will act as a key interface between Automation, MES, IT and Operations teams to ensure reliable integration, performance and continuous improvement of site manufacturing systems. The role will support the full system lifecycle including platform administration, equipment integration, change management, qualification activities and troubleshooting of manufacturing information systems. **Job Responsibilities** * Provide technical support and ownership for manufacturing platforms including MES, OPC interfaces, industrial data historians and integrated automation systems. * Support the integration of process equipment and control systems with MES and site digital platforms using OPC and related technologies. * Configure, maintain and troubleshoot MES interfaces, data collection systems and historian solutions such as AVEVA PI. * Lead and support change control activities, including impact assessments, implementation planning and documentation updates. * Develop, execute and review qualification documentation, including test protocols, commissioning activities and validation deliverables in accordance with GMP requirements. * Investigate and resolve platform issues, performing root cause analysis and implementing sustainable corrective actions to ensure system reliability. * Collaborate with Automation, CSV, IT, Engineering and Operations teams to support new equipment integrations, upgrades and site projects. * Support the lifecycle management of manufacturing systems, including patching, upgrades, periodic reviews and obsolescence planning. * Maintain system documentation, including design specifications, configuration records, SOPs and support procedures. * Identify and implement continuous improvement initiatives to enhance data integrity, system performance and manufacturing efficiency. **Job Requirements** * Bachelor's degree in Automation, Electrical, Computer, Chemical Engineering, Information Systems, or a related technical discipline. * Minimum 5 years' experience supporting manufacturing, automation, or digital platforms within a pharmaceutical or other GMP\-regulated environment. * Experience supporting and integrating Manufacturing Execution Systems (MES) with plant equipment and control systems. * Strong understanding of industrial communication protocols, particularly OPC technologies. * Hands\-on experience with industrial data historians, preferably AVEVA PI. * Knowledge of GMP requirements, computerized system lifecycle management, and change control processes. * Experience authoring and executing qualification and validation documentation, including IQ/OQ protocols and testing activities. * Familiarity with PLC, HMI, and SCADA technologies, with exposure to platforms such as Allen\-Bradley considered advantageous. * Strong troubleshooting, problem\-solving, and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to work effectively across Automation, IT, Validation, Engineering, and Operations teams.

title: People Operations Administrator
description: **Title** : People Operations Administrator **Location:** Dundalk HQ (Hybrid) **Reports To:** People Operations Manager **Job Description** The People Operations Administrator plays a key role within the People Operations function, supporting the effective delivery of HR administration, employee lifecycle processes, HR systems management and reporting across BOYLE Sports. Working within a fast\-paced and operationally complex environment, the role is responsible for maintaining accurate employee data, supporting HR compliance processes, coordinating onboarding and offboarding activity, supporting payroll\-related administration and ensuring a high standard of service delivery to employees, managers and the wider People team. The role acts as a key point of contact for employee and manager queries while also supporting wider People projects, process improvements, reporting requirements and HR systems activities across multiple business areas and jurisdictions. The successful candidate will work closely with People Business Partners, Payroll, Talent Acquisition, L\&D, Reward, Operations and line managers to support efficient and compliant people processes across the organisation. **Responsibilities** * Maintain and administer employee records across HR systems ensuring accuracy, integrity and GDPR compliance at all times. * Support the administration of the employee lifecycle including onboarding, contractual changes, transfers and offboarding processes. * Coordinate and process new starter documentation, system set up and onboarding administration. * Support critical People Operation processes which impact payroll, including employee changes, absence tracking, benefits administration and maintaining accurate employee data and reporting. * Support the administration and monitoring of annual leave, sickness absence, maternity leave and other employee leave processes. * Produce and maintain HR reports, people metrics and data analysis to support operational and business requirements. * Support managers and employees with HR systems queries and process guidance. * Assist with HR compliance administration including right to work documentation, probation tracking, policy acknowledgements and employee records management. * Support employee benefits administration including pension, healthcare and Death in Service\-related processes. * Assist with maintaining and improving People Operations workflows, trackers and administrative processes. * Support HR audits, compliance reviews and reporting requirements as required. * Assist with HR Support system implementation, testing, process improvement and system enhancement projects. * Support the wider People team with administration relating to employee relations, organisational changes, restructures and business projects where required. * Build effective working relationships with managers and stakeholders across multiple business functions. * Ensure a professional, responsive and employee\-focused approach to all People Operations activities. **Requirements** * Previous experience working within a busy HR, People Operations or HR administration environment would be considered an asset. * Strong organisational and administrative skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment. * Exceptional attention to detail and accuracy, particularly when working with employee data and reporting. * Experience working with HR systems and reporting tools would be considered an asset. * Strong communication and stakeholder management skills. * Ability to handle confidential and sensitive information professionally. * Good understanding of HR processes and employment legislation would be preferred. * Strong Microsoft Office skills, particularly Excel and reporting tools would be preferred * Ability to work collaboratively across teams and functions. * Experience supporting payroll or benefits administration would be advantageous. * Experience working within a multi\-site or operational environment would be beneficial. **Company:** BOYLE Sports is an international sports betting and gaming company, with an extensive online business and retail portfolio. Founded by John Boyle in 1982, the Irish family\-owned firm has grown to become Ireland’s largest and most successful independently\-owned bookmaker and has over 390 shops across Ireland and the UK. Its Head Office is located on the outskirts of Dundalk in the Republic of Ireland and the company currently employs over 2,700 employees across Ireland, the UK and Gibraltar. BOYLE Sports offers a world class betting and gaming experience with a ‘Customer First’ approach, committed to bringing customers closer to the action. Its mission is boosted by a rich sponsorship portfolio – the company is a proud principal sponsor of West Ham United Football Club and it stays at the heart of the action all year round by backing some of sport’s most prestigious events, including the Irish Grand National, the Irish Greyhound Derby and the World Grand Prix of Darts.

title: Personal Assistant
description: On behalf of our client in Dublin 2, we are recruiting for a PA for a permanent position in a professional organisation where confidentiality and diplomacy are essential. **Key duties will include:** * Extensive diary management and scheduling across multiple time zones * Being the first point of contact for internal and external clients * Organising travel and managing consultant expenses * Preparing complex documentation to demanding deadlines * Assisting with the smooth running of the office and liaising with other offices/IT/Finance and various internal and external stakeholders as necessary * Helping to organise external and internal events and meetings and maintain the accuracy of our CRM database **About you:** * You will have at least three years’ experience of working in a similar role \- previous experience in professional services would be advantageous but not essential. * Confident, deadline driven and focused on delivery * Proactive with a positive outlook and a flexible manner, friendly and helpful * A good team player, client\-focused with high attention to detail * Able to work under pressure and juggle simultaneous projects * Well\-versed in using Microsoft Outlook, Word, Excel and PowerPoint, and able to touch\-type * Able to learn and use the internal database as a project management tool and archive system On offer is a competitive salary plus a discretionary bonus \& benefits.

title: Platform Engineer
description: **About Us:** At Sitecore, our mission is to simplify how brands reach, engage, and serve people by delivering intelligent, personalised digital experiences that connect the world. We empower the world’s most iconic brands to build lifelong relationships with their customers—seamlessly, smartly, and at scale. As the leading provider of agentic digital experience software, Sitecore brings together content, commerce, and data into one composable platform that enables brands to deliver millions of meaningful, adaptive experiences every day. Trusted by global leaders such as American Express, Porsche, Starbucks, and L’Oréal, Sitecore helps brands transform engagement through experiences that are not only personalised but predictive and dynamic. Our foundation is our people—a diverse, passionate, and collaborative global team spanning over 25 countries. We believe that every experience matters, and that belief starts with how we work together. Our values guide how we lead, innovate, and connect. They are the behaviours that bring our mission and vision to life, every day, in every interaction. As we continue to evolve, we are actively cultivating AI skills across our teams to unlock new levels of creativity, efficiency, and insight. From engineering to customer experience, AI capabilities are becoming integral to how we design, build, and deliver the next generation of digital experiences. Learn more at Sitecore.com. **About the Role:** We’re looking for a Platform Engineer to build and operate our cloud infrastructure and developer platform capabilities. You’ll work as part of a team responsible for delivering scalable, reliable, and observable systems that power our applications. This role is hands\-on and focused on implementing and maintaining Azure\-based infrastructure, Kubernetes environments, Infrastructure as Code, and observability tooling. **What You’ll Do:** * Design, deploy, and maintain cloud infrastructure on Microsoft Azure (e.g., AKS, ACR, networking, storage, policies) following established architectural standards. * Support and operate Kubernetes clusters, including deployments, scaling, upgrades, and routine troubleshooting. * Build and maintain Infrastructure as Code (IaC) using Terraform, including reusable modules and environment\-based configurations. * Implement and improve observability using Prometheus (metrics and alerting) and Grafana (dashboards and basic alerting), with optional support for Loki where applicable. * Support Linux\-based environments (Ubuntu/RHEL), including troubleshooting, basic performance tuning, and security best practices. * Collaborate with developers and senior engineers to improve deployment workflows, platform reliability, and internal tooling. * Participate in on\-call rotations and assist in incident response and post\-incident follow\-ups. * Contribute to documentation, automation, and platform improvement initiatives. **What You Need to Succeed:** * 3–5 years of experience in platform, cloud, DevOps, or infrastructure engineering roles. * Solid understanding of Microsoft Azure fundamentals and core services. * Practical experience working with Kubernetes (deployments, Helm, services, basic networking and scaling concepts). * Experience using Terraform for infrastructure provisioning (modules and multi\-environment setups preferred). * Good Linux administration skills (processes, networking basics, logging, troubleshooting). * Familiarity with monitoring and observability tools such as Prometheus and Grafana. * Basic scripting ability in Bash or Python. * Understanding of version control (Git) and CI/CD concepts. * Strong willingness to learn, collaborate, and improve engineering practices. **Additional Skills That Could Set You Apart:** * Exposure to AWS services (e.g., EC2, EKS, IAM). * Familiarity with observability tools such as Loki, OpenTelemetry, or ELK stack. * Exposure to GitOps tools (ArgoCD, Flux) or policy\-as\-code concepts (OPA/Gatekeeper). * Interest in infrastructure automation and platform engineering best practices. **What We Offer** * Opportunity to own critical platform components end\-to\-end * Modern toolset and high\-impact work (Azure \+ K8s \+ observability focus) * Collaborative, high\-performing team environment **Equal Opportunities at Sitecore** Sitecore is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace. We are committed to equal employment opportunity without unlawful regard to race, colour, ancestry, religion, gender, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, veteran status or any other local legally protected characteristic. **\#HYBRID**

title: Platform Engineer
description: IT Contracting has gotten a boost with a great Platform Engineering opportunity with one of our clients in their Dublin offices! **About Your New Employer** * You will be working with one of the world's leading financial institutions, serving individual consumers, small and middle\-market businesses, and large corporations with a full range of banking, investing, asset management, and other financial and risk management products and services. * They are known for their commitment to innovation and technology, providing cutting\-edge digital banking solutions and services to enhance customer experience and streamline financial operations. * They are dedicated to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace, promoting a culture where employees from all backgrounds can thrive and contribute to the company's success. **About Your New Job as a Platform Engineer** * You will work as a key contributor within the cloud platform team, shaping and supporting robust cloud\-native environments in AWS. * Actively design, automate, and maintain secure, production\-grade infrastructure leveraging Infrastructure as Code (Terraform) and CI/CD pipelines. * Collaborate with security, engineering, and operations teams to deliver well\-architected cloud platforms aligned with industry and regulatory best practices. * Lead on cloud security, identity management, and networking, including implementation of zero\-trust architecture, governance, data encryption, and multi\-account setups. * Support cost and performance optimisation across diverse, scalable cloud environments and drive innovation through the adoption of automated workflows. . **What Skills You Need in this job as a Platform Engineer** * Senior\-level experience in software/platform engineering or site reliability engineering, including direct AWS experience. * Proven expertise in building and operating cloud environments in regulated industries; experience in cloud security, networking, identity/access management, and governance. * Deep understanding of modern networking concepts (firewalls, DNS, Load Balancing, Proxies) and proficiency with a scripting/programming language (Python, Go, or Shell). * Strong experience with Infrastructure as Code (Terraform) and cloud automation, plus hands\-on delivery of CI/CD pipelines (GitOps). * Familiarity with multi\-account AWS architectures, containerisation (Docker), Kubernetes/EKS, monitoring tools (Grafana, Prometheus, Splunk), and AWS native monitoring. * Excellent communication skills, with a collaborative and problem\-solving mindset. **What’s on Offer in this job as a Platform Engineer** * Daily Rate of up to €500\+ * Hybrid working environment that is 3 days a week in the office * Good likelihood of contract extensions **What’s Next** If you think this job is for you, apply now! Looking forward to talking to some great Platform Engineers!

title: Power Platform Developer
description: Our Dublin\-based client is looking for a Power Platform Developer to join their team. This position is with a well\-established, market\-leading organisation. For this role, you will be required to work on\-site. **Key Responsibilities** * Design, develop, and maintain solutions using the Microsoft Power Platform, including Power Apps, Power Automate, and Power BI. * Build and customise Canvas Apps and Model\-Driven Apps to support and improve business processes. * Develop automated workflows and integrations using Power Automate. * Collaborate with business stakeholders to gather requirements and translate them into functional and technical solutions. * Integrate Power Platform solutions with Dynamics 365, SharePoint Online, Microsoft 365, Azure services, and third\-party applications. * Support, maintain, and enhance existing Power Platform applications and workflows. * Design and develop SharePoint Online solutions, including lists, libraries, forms, and collaboration sites. * Ensure solutions are scalable, secure, and aligned with best practices and governance standards. * Create reports, dashboards, and business insights using Power BI. * Participate in testing, deployment, and continuous improvement activities across the application lifecycle. * Ensure all solutions comply with security, compliance, and data governance Dynamics 365, SharePoint Online, Microsoft 365, and external data sources. * Knowledge of Dataverse and data modelling principles. * Experience with Azure services and API integrations. * Understanding of solution lifecycle management (ALM) and deployment processes. * Experience using Azure DevOps or similar project management and CI/CD tools. * Strong analytical, problem\-solving, and communication skills. * Knowledge of software development lifecycle (SDLC) methodologies and best practices. * Familiarity with database technologies and data integration techniques. * Relevant title: Process Engineer

title: Procurement Specialist
description: **Procurement Specialist** The position is hybrid role office based in the Dublin Area **Who We Are:** With 20\-years of proven growth and exceptional performance, our mission is to be our customer’s best service provider. We realize the importance of diversity in achieving that goal. Our company was built upon a solid foundation of 10 Values and Beliefs which drive our unmatched culture, making us the \#1 global, serviced\-based systems integrator in the industry. We take great pride in protecting the lives and assets of our customers and their communities with the solutions we provide. **Position Summary:** The procurement specialist provides procurement support to Convergint Operations Directors/Managers, Project and Site Managers and/or others as required. Full control of all Project, Smallworks, Service and LSPs procurement activities, and the main point of contact for suppliers and Convergint colleagues within a Procurement functionality. The procurement specialist supports the delivery of service and project\-based solutions by executing day\-to\-day procurement activities for materials, equipment and subcontracted services. Operating in a Global service integrator environment with scope in Europe locations, this role focuses on transactional purchasing, supplier/partner coordination, and procurement to pay process execution, ensuring timely availability of materials and services required for all customer project and support activities. **Job Responsibilities:** **Procurement Process:** * Procurement of all Materials and Subcontractor POs for Operations Projects. * Procurement of all Materials for Small works \& Service\-related works. * Raising POs for System training on behalf of Technical Manager/Service Engineers. * Raising POs for IT Stock and subscriptions on behalf of IT Department. * Execute purchase orders for project materials, subcontracted services, etc, based on approved requisitions, ensuring alignment between purchase orders, project budgets, and delivery schedules and responding to urgent operational and service\-critical procurement needs. **Supplier/Partner Coordination:** * Act as the procurement operational point of contact for suppliers/partners * Confirm order acknowledgements, lead times, delivery dates, and site\-specific requirements. * Managing day\-to\-day Account Management with Suppliers/Partners at local / regional level * Negotiating project pricing to help drive profitability and support operational negotiations on pricing, lead times, delivery terms, and service scope within defined authority. **Compliance \& Documentation:** * Manage and Maintain savings tracker. * Managing and reporting supplier SLAs to Procurement Manager Europe and Head of Procurement \& Logistics Europe. * Ensure purchases comply with internal procurement policies. * Maintain record for project and service procurement, including POs, confirmations, and delivery records. **Cross Functional Collaboration:** * After sales support, assisting Logistics in the Returns Process when needed. * Assisting the AE/Design Team when needed on providing strategic solutions / products to the business. * Liaise with Key stakeholders to ensure materials are purchased and delivered within budget and to the set timescales. * Reordering of inventory, Van Stock and Health \& Safety equipment alongside Warehouse colleagues. * Escalate supply risks or delays that could impact customer delivery or contractual commitments. * Work closely with project managers, service managers, technicians, and warehouse/logistics teams. **Continuous Improvement, Operational Support, System Accuracy \& Data Quality:** * Monitor stock trends and provide forecasts available to meet project demands. * Reordering of inventory, Van Stock and Health \& Safety equipment alongside Warehouse colleagues. * Training other departments on the Procurement Process/ New ERP System. * Liaising with other CTCs on Stock availability and queries. * Use different Europe ERPs and procurement systems consistently for PO creation, service entry * sheets, and confirmations, maintaining accurate purchasing records linked to projects, service orders, and cost centers. * Identify and escalate pricing, supplier master data, or system issues that impact procurement * execution. * Identify recurring procurement issues affecting project or service execution and propose * improvements. * Support rollout and adoption of standardized procurement processes and tools across Europe. * Contribute to operational area KPIs. * Liaising with the Accounts/Invoicing Departments and assisting where needed on invoice queries and ICO Orders. **Other Ad hoc requests when needed** **Job Skills Requirements:** * Strong communication written and oral, and personal customer service skills and ability to work under pressure. * Solid Microsoft Outlook, Excel, and Word skills. * Solid organizational skills and the ability to handle multiple projects tasks simultaneously * Excellent attention to detail and data accuracy. * Solid verbal, written and interpersonal communication skills. * Effective communication with suppliers/partners, and internal stakeholders. * Solid ability to facilitate a collaborative working environment for customers and team members. * Ability to use multiple ERP systems. * Strong analytical skills. * Fluency in English; additional European languages are an advantage. **Education Requirements** : * High School diploma or equivalent. NVQ (National Vocational Qualification) in Business administration * Bachelor’s degree in supply chain, Logistics, Business Administration, or equivalent experience (a Plus) **Experience Requirements:** * \+ 2 of procurement or strategic sourcing experience, project manager, ideally within security systems, technology systems integration, distributors or related technical industries (a plus). * Previous Procurement and supplier relationship experience preferred. * Previous experience in Supplier evaluation and best practices in buying. * Experience in a service integrator, system integrator, or project\-based environment is strongly preferred. * Hands\-on experience with ERP/P2P systems and service\-based procurement workflows.

title: Production Operator
description: For 75 years, Charles River employees have worked together to assist in the discovery, development and safe manufacture of new drug therapies. When you join our family, you will have a significant impact on the health and well\-being of people across the globe. Whether your background is in life sciences, finance, IT, sales or another area, your skills will play an important role in the work we perform. In return, we’ll help you build a career that you can feel passionate about. **Job Description** We are seeking a Production Operator to join our team at our Rathcoole site in D24 NF21 Co.Dublin. This role is Full time, Monday to Friday 08:30 to 16:30 (with occasional early finsh on Friday) 37\.5 hours per week, It is an On\-Site role and the ideal candidate will have a vehicle and Full Licence or live in a commutable location close to D24 Eirecode. This role comes with Salary, Paid Holidays, Pension Plan, Medical Insurance and other great employee benefits. This role is Ideally suited to someone who enjoys active, physical work and has some demonstrable track record of performance within a similar environment. What you'll be doing * Operate to a high standard under all established SOP \& procedures. * Carry out all aspects of kitting according to standard operation procedure. * Maintain all work areas in an orderly fashion as per SOP. * Perform all LAL Production related activities including, glassware preparation, cleaning, labeling, packaging \& SAP transactions. * Responsible for labeling of non\-licensed products, final product inspection, preparation and finishing of accessory products, and general environmental upkeep. * Prepare, maintain, and distribute appropriate documentation for all materials kitted \& shipped. * GDP compliant. * Coordinate with Planning department \& Logistics Management to ensure all work is performed as required. * Perform all other related duties as assigned by Manager or Supervisor. **Role Requirements And Qualifications** * Education: High school diploma or Equivalent. * Experience: experience in a similar working environment. * An equivalent combination of education and experience may be accepted as a satisfactory substitute for the specific education and experience listed above. * Other: Must have excellent organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple priorities; attention to detail, computer literacy; and the ability to interact appropriately at all levels. * Ideally Awareness of supply chain processes and best practices. * SAP experience desirable but not essential. **About Biologics Testing Solutions** With more than 50 years of experience and proven regulatory expertise, the Charles River Biologics group can address challenging projects for biotechnology and pharmaceutical companies worldwide. Offering a variety of services such as contamination and impurity testing, protein characterzation, bioassays, viral clearance studies and stability and lot release programs, we support clients throughout the biologic development cycle, from the establishment and characterization of cell banks through preclinical and clinical studies to marketed products. Whether clients need stand\-alone services, a unique package of testing, or insourced support, our Biologics group can create a custom solution to suit their needs. Each year more than 20,000 biologic testing reports are sent each and over 200 licenses products are supported by our biologics testing solutions team. **About Charles River** Charles River is an early\-stage contract research organization (CRO). We have built upon our foundation of laboratory animal medicine and science to develop a diverse portfolio of discovery and safety assessment services, both Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) and non\-GLP, to support clients from target identification through preclinical development. Charles River also provides a suite of products and services to support our clients’ clinical laboratory testing needs and manufacturing activities. Utilizing this broad portfolio of products and services enables our clients to create a more flexible drug development model, which reduces their costs, enhances their productivity and effectiveness to increase speed to market. With over 20,000 employees within 110 facilities in over 20 countries around the globe, we are strategically positioned to coordinate worldwide resources and apply multidisciplinary perspectives in resolving our client’s unique challenges. Our client base includes global pharmaceutical companies, biotechnology companies, government agencies and hospitals and academic institutions around the world. At Charles River, we are passionate about our role in improving the quality of people’s lives. Our mission, our excellent science and our strong sense of purpose guide us in all that we do, and we approach each day with the knowledge that our work helps to improve the health and well\-being of many across the globe. We have proudly supported the development of 80% of the drugs approved by the FDA in the last five years. At Charles River Laboratories, we recognize and recruit all talent. We are a company committed to the principle of diversity and inclusion and work daily in this direction. 233926

title: Professional Support Lawyer (Knowledge) — Projects, Infrastructure & Energy
description: **About Our Client** Our client is a leading, progressive law firm looking to welcome a Professional Support Lawyer (Knowledge) to their multi\-disciplinary Projects, Infrastructure \& Energy group. This is a unique, dynamic role that perfectly blends knowledge leadership with active legal practice. You will enjoy the variety of a strategic knowledge role while staying close to live client matters by carrying a manageable, reduced fee\-earning caseload. **The Role Overview** As a critical support hub for a busy and growing practice, you will provide technical know\-how, training, and support across a broad spectrum of areas, including: * Construction \& engineering * Public procurement * Energy \& natural resources * Planning \& environment * Housing \& ESG (including occasional support for the New Homes/Residential Development team) If you are a qualified solicitor who possesses a passion for technical accuracy, communicates complex legal points with clarity, and thrives on helping colleagues succeed, this role offers an exceptional platform for your career. **Key Responsibilities** * **Knowledge Management:** Develop, curate, and continuously improve the group's knowledge systems, document banks, precedents, and template advice across all core practice areas. * **Legal Analysis:** Monitor and analyze Irish and EU legislative, regulatory, and case\-law developments, translating them into practical guidance for fee earners. * **Training \& Professional Support:** Design and deliver internal training and CPD briefings for legal staff at all levels, while mentoring junior fee earners. * **Fee\-Earning Work:** Maintain a defined, manageable caseload of live matters alongside the wider team, billing in line with a reduced fee target that respects your dual responsibilities. * **Cross\-Departmental Collaboration:** Act as the first point of contact for technical queries, and collaborate with knowledge colleagues across the firm to share resources and best practices. * **Business Development:** Contribute to client\-facing articles, briefings, seminars, and thought\-leadership materials to help elevate the group's profile. **Person Specification** **Essential Criteria:** * Qualified Solicitor (Republic of Ireland) with relevant post\-qualification experience in one or more of the group's practice areas. * Strong technical research and analytical skills, with the ability to distil complex legal material into practical advice. * Exceptional drafting skills and an uncompromising eye for detail. * Excellent communication and presentation skills, with the confidence to deliver training. * Highly organized, self\-directed, and collaborative. **Desirable Criteria:** * Previous experience in a Professional Support Lawyer (PSL) or Knowledge role. * Familiarity with standard\-form construction contracts, public procurement procedures, or residential development. * Experience designing and delivering training or CPD programs. * Comfort utilizing legal technology, knowledge systems, and document automation tools. **What’s on Offer?** * **Broad Exposure:** Work across a multi\-disciplinary practice involved in significant Irish infrastructure, energy, and housing projects. * **The Best of Both Worlds:** A rare role that balances strategic knowledge leadership with continued hands\-on legal work. * **Modern Flexibility:** A supportive hybrid working model. Scope for Part Time. * **Reward:** A highly competitive remuneration and benefits package within a collegiate, growth\-oriented environment. If interested, apply directly or reach out to Caitriona Breaw @ Amicus Recruitment for a more detailed conversation.

title: Property Management Surveyor
description: **JLL empowers you to shape a brighter way**. Our people at JLL are shaping the future of real estate for a better world by combining world class services, advisory and technology for our clients. We are committed to hiring the best, most talented people and empowering them to thrive, grow meaningful careers and to find a place where they belong. Whether you’ve got deep experience in commercial real estate, skilled trades or technology, or you’re looking to apply your relevant experience to a new industry, join our team as we help shape a brighter way forward. Job Description**Surveyor \- Property Management** **Grade P2** ------------ **About JLL** ------------- **JLL shapes the future of real estate through world\-class services, advisory, and technology. The Surveyor role in Property Management (PM) team focuses on delivering exceptional property management, contributing to the Centre of Excellence, and maintaining strong client relationships.** -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- **The Role** This role is a high profile Retail Management Role working a mixed portfolio, including a trophy shopping centre (Cabot Circus) for 2 key JLL clients, Hammerson and L\&G. **Office location** The role is based in Bristol and will sit within JLL’s Property Delivery Team. **Key Responsibilities** ------------------------ ### **Client \& Stakeholder Support** * Support the Property lead as a key contact for the Asset Manager across allocated assets. * Maintain professional and responsive communication with clients, occupiers, and stakeholders. * Assist in preparing client reports and attending meetings where required. ### **Lease and Legal Management** * Assist in receiving, progressing, and preparing recommendations on occupier applications (licence to alter, wayleaves, underletting, assignments). * Monitor tenant compliance with lease covenants and obligations, escalating issues and supporting resolution. * Respond to routine technical queries in relation to leases and licences, referring more complex matters where appropriate. * Support resolution of occupier matters that may influence asset performance or valuation. ### **Financial Management** * Support financial processes including raising charges, monitoring income, and liaising with accounts teams. * Assist with arrears management in collaboration with credit control teams. * Maintain accurate financial records in line with internal processes and client requirements. ### **Service Charge** * Assist in the preparation and management of service charge budgets, reconciliations, and cash flow forecasts. * Ensure compliance with RICS guidelines and support achievement of client KPIs. ### **ESG** * Support the delivery of ESG initiatives in collaboration with FM and specialist teams. * Assist in implementing client ESG strategies across the portfolio. ### **Supplier \& Facilities Management** * Support management of third\-party suppliers, monitoring performance and escalating issues where necessary. * Work collaboratively with colleagues to ensure service delivery meets required standards. ### **Inspections** * Undertake regular property inspections, ensuring assets are well presented and issues are identified, recorded, and progressed for resolution. ### **Transactions / Transitions** * Support property transitions, including onboarding and offboarding assets and assisting with due diligence processes. * Liaise with solicitors and third parties under the direction of the Associate. ### **Occupier Engagement \& Customer Experience** * Develop positive relationships with occupiers and support initiatives to enhance customer satisfaction. * Gather occupier intelligence and feedback for reporting to the client. * Assist in the delivery of customer experience initiatives across the portfolio. ### **Works** * Support delivery of major works and site\-based projects, working with consultants, contractors, and internal teams. ### **Health \& Safety** * Maintain an understanding of relevant Health \& Safety legislation and support compliance across properties. ### **Insurance** * Assist with the insurance renewal process and day\-to\-day insurance matters. * Support administration of insurance claims through to resolution. ### **Void Property Management** * Support management of vacant units, ensuring compliance with client and insurer requirements. ### **Fees** * Assist in capturing and tracking fee\-earning opportunities in line with agreed processes. **Additional Responsibilities** ------------------------------- * Support business development activities and identify opportunities to enhance client relationships. * Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives within the team. * Assist with project\-based work (internal and external). * Identify and help resolve legacy property management issues. **Candidate Profile** --------------------- * Ideally working towards MRICS or recently qualified. * Solid experience in property management or related role. * Good understanding of landlord and tenant matters. * Strong organisational skills with attention to detail. * Commercially aware with a proactive approach to problem solving. * Strong communication skills and ability to build relationships. * Competent IT skills, including property management systems. * Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines. **Behavioural Skills** ---------------------- * Collaborative team player supporting colleagues and wider team objectives. * Professional and confident communicator (internal and external). * Willingness to learn and develop technical expertise. * Positive, proactive, and solution\-oriented mindset. * Adheres to JLL working practices and values. * Contributes to an inclusive and supportive team culture. **Location:** On\-site –Dublin, IRL If this job description resonates with you, we encourage you to apply even if you don’t meet all of the requirements. We’re interested in getting to know you and what you bring to the table! At JLL, we harness the power of artificial intelligence (AI) to efficiently accelerate meaningful connections between candidates and opportunities. title: Property Management Surveyor
description: **Job Description** **Surveyor \- Property Management** **Grade P2** **About JLL** JLL shapes the future of real estate through world\-class services, advisory, and technology. The Surveyor role in Property Management (PM) team focuses on delivering exceptional property management, contributing to the Centre of Excellence, and maintaining strong client relationships. **The Role** This role is a high profile Retail Management Role working a mixed portfolio, including a trophy shopping centre (Cabot Circus) for 2 key JLL clients, Hammerson and L\&G. **Office location** The role is based in Bristol and will sit within JLL’s Property Delivery Team. **Key Responsibilities** **Client \& Stakeholder Support** * Support the Property lead as a key contact for the Asset Manager across allocated assets. * Maintain professional and responsive communication with clients, occupiers, and stakeholders. * Assist in preparing client reports and attending meetings where required. **Lease and Legal Management** * Assist in receiving, progressing, and preparing recommendations on occupier applications (licence to alter, wayleaves, underletting, assignments). * Monitor tenant compliance with lease covenants and obligations, escalating issues and supporting resolution. * Respond to routine technical queries in relation to leases and licences, referring more complex matters where appropriate. * Support resolution of occupier matters that may influence asset performance or valuation. **Financial Management** * Support financial processes including raising charges, monitoring income, and liaising with accounts teams. * Assist with arrears management in collaboration with credit control teams. * Maintain accurate financial records in line with internal processes and client requirements. **Service Charge** * Assist in the preparation and management of service charge budgets, reconciliations, and cash flow forecasts. * Ensure compliance with RICS guidelines and support achievement of client KPIs. **ESG** * Support the delivery of ESG initiatives in collaboration with FM and specialist teams. * Assist in implementing client ESG strategies across the portfolio. **Supplier \& Facilities Management** * Support management of third\-party suppliers, monitoring performance and escalating issues where necessary. * Work collaboratively with colleagues to ensure service delivery meets required standards. **Inspections** * Undertake regular property inspections, ensuring assets are well presented and issues are identified, recorded, and progressed for resolution. **Transactions / Transitions** * Support property transitions, including onboarding and offboarding assets and assisting with due diligence processes. * Liaise with solicitors and third parties under the direction of the Associate. **Occupier Engagement \& Customer Experience** * Develop positive relationships with occupiers and support initiatives to enhance customer satisfaction. * Gather occupier intelligence and feedback for reporting to the client. * Assist in the delivery of customer experience initiatives across the portfolio. **Works** * Support delivery of major works and site\-based projects, working with consultants, contractors, and internal teams. **Health \& Safety** * Maintain an understanding of relevant Health \& Safety legislation and support compliance across properties. **Insurance** * Assist with the insurance renewal process and day\-to\-day insurance matters. * Support administration of insurance claims through to resolution. **Void Property Management** * Support management of vacant units, ensuring compliance with client and insurer requirements. **Fees** * Assist in capturing and tracking fee\-earning opportunities in line with agreed processes. **Additional Responsibilities** * Support business development activities and identify opportunities to enhance client relationships. * Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives within the team. * Assist with project\-based work (internal and external). * Identify and help resolve legacy property management issues. **Candidate Profile** * Ideally working towards MRICS or recently qualified. * Solid experience in property management or related role. * Good understanding of landlord and tenant matters. * Strong organisational skills with attention to detail. * Commercially aware with a proactive approach to problem solving. * Strong communication skills and ability to build relationships. * Competent IT skills, including property management systems. * Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines. **Behavioural Skills** * Collaborative team player supporting colleagues and wider team objectives. * Professional and confident communicator (internal and external). * Willingness to learn and develop technical expertise. * Positive, proactive, and solution\-oriented mindset. * Adheres to JLL working practices and values. * Contributes to an inclusive and supportive team culture.
